user_message:
Read-only third-pass design review. Do NOT edit any files. Return feedback as text only. <task> Perform a third-pass read-only design review against the plan at /Users/teslamint/.claude/plans/robust-humming-papert.md and the codebase at /Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext. GitHub issue #41 (milestone v0.2.0). Section A — Verify previous findings are resolved (label each RESOLVED / PARTIALLY RESOLVED / NOT RESOLVED with citation to plan section): A.B3: checkpoint.files_snapshot NULL/format ambiguity — was the columns NULL-by-default reality and dict|list ambiguity addressed? Check Source-specific signal logic table and Context notes.

A.B10: hook pre-launch gate gap — does the plan broaden the gate so sessions without keyword-matching summaries but with checkpoint/assessment signals still reach the worker? Check Pipeline Architecture and hook tests.

A.B11: create_decision signature — does the confirm path call it with rejected_alternatives as a keyword argument, not as the 5th positional? Check Confirmation flow step 1.

A.B5: dogfood config framing — is .entirecontext/config.toml removed from Files to modify and reframed as operator-only? Check Files to modify / create and Verification.

A.B6: telemetry conn positional — are record_operation_event(...) calls shown with conn as first positional? Check Pipeline Architecture and Confirmation flow step 4.

A.B8: test side-effect migration — does the plan enumerate which existing tests assert side effects and map each to its new assertion? Check Backwards compatibility and test migration.

A.A2: real-session fixture automation — does the plan include real sanitized session fixtures (not just handcrafted), with a reproducible generation method?

A.A10: typed error class — is DecisionExtractionError (or equivalent) explicitly added to the new module, matching core/search.py / core/purge.py convention?

Section B — Verify new pieces against actual code. Read these files before commenting:
- src/entirecontext/db/schema.py (sessions, turns, checkpoints, assessments, decisions, decision_*, operation_events, FTS table definitions and triggers; schema version 12)
- src/entirecontext/core/decisions.py lines 187-195 (create_decision), 571-663 (link helpers), 950-1026 (FTS tokenizer and rank normalizer)
- src/entirecontext/core/checkpoint.py — how files_snapshot is written (or not)
- src/entirecontext/core/telemetry.py:200-265 — record_operation_event signature and VALID_OPERATION_STATUSES
- src/entirecontext/core/security.py or wherever filter_secrets lives
- src/entirecontext/core/content_filter.py — confirm redact_for_query or equivalent sanitizer exists for tier-2 fixture generator
- src/entirecontext/hooks/decision_hooks.py:700-774 — current maybe_extract_decisions + _summaries_match_keywords + _session_has_extraction_marker
- src/entirecontext/hooks/session_lifecycle.py:290, 350-456 — SessionEnd hook and auto-checkpoint creation paths
- src/entirecontext/cli/decisions_cmds.py:460-588 — shim symbols the plan preserves
- tests/test_decision_hooks.py:1300-1450 — seven test methods being migrated
- tests/test_decisions_cli.py:470-590 — four monkeypatch sites being migrated
- tests/conftest.py — fixture helpers

Then check:

B1 (Turn window derivation): Does turns have created_at and checkpoints have created_at making the window query unambiguous? Or do we need checkpoint_id on turns? If underspecified, checkpoint-source tests will be flaky.

B2 (_session_has_checkpoint_signal SQL): Does checkpoints.diff_summary store reliable non-empty text, or is empty-string-on-zero-diff common? If empty-string is common, the gate fires when it shouldnt. B3 (_session_has_assessment_signal SQL): Is assessments.checkpoint_id populated by the real assessment creation flow in core/futures.py and cli/futures_cmds.py? If assessments exist without checkpoint links, theyre invisible to this gate.

B4 (Metadata marker rename shim): Existing tests at test_decision_hooks.py:1344-1346, 1387-1389 assert meta.get(decisions_extracted) is True. After migration, new tests check candidates_extracted. What happens to real-world v12 session metadata in the dev DB — will they re-extract instead of being skipped?

B5 (Assessment source file derivation): If the assessment is linked to a checkpoint with no earlier checkpoint (first checkpoint), the window is empty. The plan says files=[], but should it use all turns up to this checkpoint?

B6 (FTS5 trigger definitions): Does the existing fts_decisions trigger pattern use content-table style or automatic triggers? The new fts_decision_candidates table must mirror exactly. Cite the actual trigger definition from schema.py.

B7 (Typed error class placement): If DecisionExtractionError is declared in the new extraction module, is it re-exported somewhere the hook and CLI can import it cleanly?

B8 (Scope creep check): Is the real-session fixture sampler its own mini-project that should be deferred?

B9 (Anything else): Any silent assumption about a helper or table column that doesnt exist, Python signature mismatches against what core/decisions.py actually exports, or test-migration collisions with unstated couplings. </task> <structured_output_contract> Return numbered findings (A.B3, A.B10, A.B11, A.B5, A.B6, A.B8, A.A2, A.A10, B1, B2, B3, B4, B5, B6, B7, B8, B9) with severity Critical / Important / Suggestion. For each finding cite specific plan section or file:line. Label Section A items RESOLVED / PARTIALLY RESOLVED / NOT RESOLVED. Be strict. If a section is clean, say no new findings explicitly rather than inventing padding. Target 900-1400 words total. </structured_output_contract> <grounding_rules> Every finding must cite a specific code location (file:line) or plan section. Do not present inferences as facts. Label hypotheses clearly. Ground all claims in repository artifacts, not assumptions. Do not flag pre-existing issues unrelated to this plan. Only submit a finding if you can point to a specific code path causing the issue. </grounding_rules> <default_follow_through_policy> Read all listed files before forming any conclusion. Do not skip any file. Do not edit any files. Return only text feedback. </default_follow_through_policy>
assistant_summary: 요청 범위는 `robust-humming-papert.md` 계획과 지정된 코드/테스트 파일을 모두 읽고, 섹션 A/B 항목별로 엄격한 read-only 검토를 하는 것입니다. 먼저 관련 결정/교훈 메모리를 확인한 뒤, 계획서와 지정 파일 전부를 읽고 항목별로 근거를 대조하겠습니다.
